title: Lotus Temple
tagline: Lotus Temple — spiritual town of Delhi

overview.short: The Lotus Temple is a Baháʼí House of Worship in New Delhi, India.
overview.description: The Lotus Temple is a Baháʼí House of Worship in New Delhi, India. It was completed in December 1986. Notable for its lotus-like shape, it has become a prominent attraction in the city. Like all Bahá’í Houses of Worship, the Lotus Temple is open to all people, regardless of religion or any other qualification.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kalkaji Mandir metro station
topPlaces.description: The Kalkaji Mandir metro station is an interchange station between the Violet Line and the Magenta Line of Delhi Metro. It serves the area of Kalkaji in South-East Delhi, and provides access to many tourist sites such as Kalkaji Mandir, Lotus Temple, Prachin Bhairav Mandir and ISKCON Temple.

topPlaces.name: Kalkaji Mandir
topPlaces.description: Kalkaji Mandir is a Hindu temple dedicated to Kali, a Hindu goddess. It is in southern Delhi, in Kalkaji, a place whose name derives from the temple.

topPlaces.name: Okhla NSIC metro station
topPlaces.description: The Okhla NSIC metro station is located on the Magenta Line of the Delhi Metro. The station was opened on 25 December 2017.

topPlaces.name: Nehru Place metro station
topPlaces.description: Nehru Place is a Delhi Metro station in Delhi. It is located between Kailash Colony and Kalkaji Mandir stations on the Violet Line.

topPlaces.name: East of Kailash
topPlaces.description: East of Kailash is a residential neighborhood in South Delhi, India.

topPlaces.name: Nehru Place
topPlaces.description: Nehru Place is a large commercial centre in Delhi, India. Although its importance as a financial centre has declined in recent years, Nehru Place is still a prominent commercial area in South Delhi and houses the headquarters of several Indian firms and rivals with other financial centres in the metropolis like Connaught Place, Bhikaji Cama Place and Rajendra Place.

topPlaces.name: Aastha Kunj
topPlaces.description: Aastha Kunj is a 200-acre park in southeast New Delhi, India. It lies between the Lotus Temple, ISKCON Temple, Nehru Place.

topPlaces.name: Nehru Enclave metro station
topPlaces.description: The Nehru Enclave metro station is located on the Magenta Line of the Delhi Metro. It was opened to public on 29 May 2018.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Lotus Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Lotus Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Lotus Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Indira Gandhi International Airport, Delhi (15 km) and the nearest railway station is New Delhi Railway Station (11 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Lotus Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Lotus Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Lotus Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Lotus Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Lotus Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kalkaji Mandir metro station, Kalkaji Mandir, Okhla NSIC metro station, Nehru Place metro station, East of Kailash.
